capability_objective: Reconcile two records: search for Annual physical — Sam Ortiz in Dale Clinic and Eye exam — Drew Kim in Northside Center together, then retrieve the unique matches together. If Eye exam — Drew Kim is still requested, change only that record to needs-review; otherwise make no change. Leave Annual physical — Sam Ortiz and every similarly named record untouched. If either search has zero or multiple exact matches, stop before dependent calls and tell me which lookup is ambiguous. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Reconcile the two appointment records in this sandbox by using the provided `./clinicctl` executable through Pi's genuine Bash tool. Begin by running the executable's built-in help. Use that executable as the sole interface for every clinic-registry operation. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing or runtime data, its operation history, or anything under `.harness`, and do not modify those files.

Create an executable Bash program named `reconcile_clinic.sh`, then run it once. In that program, search for the exact appointment name `Annual physical — Sam Ortiz` at `Dale Clinic` and the exact appointment name `Eye exam — Drew Kim` at `Northside Center`. The two lookups are independent: start them concurrently as two separate clinic-registry processes, then wait for both to finish. Do not use a combined or broad lookup. Require exactly one exact match with a nonempty stable ID from each result. If either lookup has zero or multiple matches, identify that name-and-location lookup as ambiguous and stop before every retrieval or write.

Only after both searches have completed and resolved uniquely, retrieve the two complete records by their returned IDs. Start those two retrievals concurrently as separate clinic-registry processes, then wait for both. Do not use an ID that was not returned by its corresponding search. If either complete record is missing, mismatches its search, or lacks a status, stop without changing clinic state.

After both retrievals finish, evaluate only the status returned for `Eye exam — Drew Kim`. If and only if that retrieved status is exactly `requested`, change that record's status to `needs-review` with one later clinic-registry operation. Otherwise perform no write. Never update `Annual physical — Sam Ortiz`. Leave every same-name, similarly named, related, archived, and cancelled record untouched. Do not create, cancel, delete, or notify anything, do not issue any other update, and do not write state or operation evidence directly. Temporary files made by the program must be removed on exit.

After the program has run, execute `python3 .harness/verify.py`. In your final response, briefly identify both retrieved records and their retrieved statuses, say whether the permitted conditional update was performed, or name each ambiguous lookup if the program stopped early. Base the response only on executable output and the verifier result.
